conversation_history.content:
1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal
  2â†’
  3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  5â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
  6â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
  7â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
  8â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.util.safeAs
  9â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.SuppressionLocatorBuilder.CommentSuppressionHint.Type.BLOCK_END
 10â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.SuppressionLocatorBuilder.CommentSuppressionHint.Type.BLOCK_START
 11â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.SuppressionLocatorBuilder.CommentSuppressionHint.Type.EOL
 12â†’import mu.KotlinLogging
 13â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 14â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiComment
 15â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiElement
 16â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtAnnotated
 17â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtAnnotationEntry
 18â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.ValueArgument
 19â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.endOffset
 20â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.startOffset
 21â†’
 22â†’/**
 23â†’ * Detects if given `ruleId` at given `offset` is suppressed.
 24â†’ */
 25â†’internal typealias SuppressionLocator = (offset: Int, ruleId: RuleId) -> Boolean
 26â†’
 27â†’internal object SuppressionLocatorBuilder {
 28â†’    /**
 29â†’     * No suppression is detected. Always returns `false`.
 30â†’     */
 31â†’    private val NO_SUPPRESSION: SuppressionLocator = { _, _ -> false }
 32â†’
 33â†’    private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 34â†’
 35â†’    /**
 36â†’     * Mapping of non-ktlint annotations to ktlint-annotation so that ktlint rules will be suppressed automatically
 37â†’     * when specific non-ktlint annotations are found. The prevents that developers have to specify multiple annotations
 38â†’     * for the same violation.
 39â†’     */
 40â†’    private val SUPPRESS_ANNOTATION_RULE_MAP =
 41â†’        mapOf(
 42â†’            // It would have been nice if the official rule id's as defined in the Rules themselves could have been used here. But that would
 43â†’            // introduce a circular dependency between the ktlint-rule-engine and the ktlint-ruleset-standard modules.
 44â†’            "EnumEntryName" to RuleId("standard:enum-entry-name-case"),
 45â†’            "RemoveCurlyBracesFromTemplate" to RuleId("standard:string-template"),
 46â†’            "ClassName" to RuleId("standard:class-naming"),
 47â†’            "FunctionName" to RuleId("standard:function-naming"),
 48â†’            "PackageName" to RuleId("standard:package-name"),
 49â†’            "PropertyName" to RuleId("standard:property-naming"),
 50â†’        )
 51â†’    private val SUPPRESS_ANNOTATIONS = setOf("Suppress", "SuppressWarnings")
 52â†’    private val SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID = RuleId("ktlint:suppress-all-rules")
 53â†’
 54â†’    /**
 55â†’     * Builds [SuppressionLocator] for given [rootNode] of AST tree.
 56â†’     */
 57â†’    fun buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(
 58â†’        rootNode: ASTNode,
 59â†’        editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 60â†’    ): SuppressionLocator {
 61â†’        val hintsList = collect(rootNode, FormatterTags.from(editorConfig))
 62â†’        return if (hintsList.isEmpty()) {
 63â†’            NO_SUPPRESSION
 64â†’        } else {
 65â†’            toSuppressedRegionsLocator(hintsList)
 66â†’        }
 67â†’    }
 68â†’
 69â†’    private fun toSuppressedRegionsLocator(hintsList: List<SuppressionHint>): SuppressionLocator =
 70â†’        { offset, ruleId ->
 71â†’            hintsList
 72â†’                .filter { offset in it.range }
 73â†’                .any { hint -> hint.disabledRuleIds.isEmpty() || hint.disabledRuleIds.contains(ruleId) }
 74â†’        }
 75â†’
 76â†’    private fun collect(
 77â†’        rootNode: ASTNode,
 78â†’        formatterTags: FormatterTags,
 79â†’    ): List<SuppressionHint> {
 80â†’        val suppressionHints = ArrayList<SuppressionHint>()
 81â†’        val commentSuppressionsHints = mutableListOf<CommentSuppressionHint>()
 82â†’        rootNode.collect { node ->
 83â†’            node
 84â†’                .takeIf { it is PsiComment }
 85â†’                ?.createSuppressionHintFromComment(formatterTags)
 86â†’                ?.let { commentSuppressionsHints.add(it) }
 87â†’
 88â†’            // Extract all Suppress annotations and create SuppressionHints
 89â†’            node
 90â†’                .psi
 91â†’                .safeAs<KtAnnotated>()
 92â†’                ?.createSuppressionHintFromAnnotations()
 93â†’                ?.let { suppressionHints.add(it) }
 94â†’        }
 95â†’
 96â†’        return suppressionHints.plus(
 97â†’            commentSuppressionsHints.toSuppressionHints(rootNode),
 98â†’        )
 99â†’    }
100â†’
101â†’    private fun ASTNode.collect(block: (node: ASTNode) -> Unit) {
102â†’        block(this)
103â†’        this
104â†’            .getChildren(null)
105â†’            .forEach { it.collect(block) }
106â†’    }
107â†’
108â†’    private fun ASTNode.createSuppressionHintFromComment(formatterTags: FormatterTags): CommentSuppressionHint? =
109â†’        if (text.startsWith("//")) {
110â†’            createSuppressionHintFromEolComment(formatterTags)
111â†’        } else {
112â†’            createSuppressionHintFromBlockComment(formatterTags)
113â†’        }
114â†’
115â†’    private fun ASTNode.createSuppressionHintFromEolComment(formatterTags: FormatterTags): CommentSuppressionHint? =
116â†’        text
117â†’            .removePrefix("//")
118â†’            .trim()
119â†’            .split(" ")
120â†’            .takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
121â†’            ?.takeIf { it[0] == KTLINT_DISABLE || it[0] == formatterTags.formatterTagOff }
122â†’            ?.let { parts ->
123â†’                CommentSuppressionHint(
124â†’                    this,
125â†’                    HashSet(parts.tailToRuleIds()),
126â†’                    EOL,
127â†’                )
128â†’            }
129â†’
130â†’    private fun ASTNode.createSuppressionHintFromBlockComment(formatterTags: FormatterTags): CommentSuppressionHint? =
131â†’        text
132â†’            .removePrefix("/*")
133â†’            .removeSuffix("*/")
134â†’            .trim()
135â†’            .split(" ")
136â†’            .takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
137â†’            ?.let { parts ->
138â†’                if (parts[0] == KTLINT_DISABLE || parts[0] == formatterTags.formatterTagOff) {
139â†’                    CommentSuppressionHint(
140â†’                        this,
141â†’                        HashSet(parts.tailToRuleIds()),
142â†’                        BLOCK_START,
143â†’                    )
144â†’                } else if (parts[0] == KTLINT_ENABLE || parts[0] == formatterTags.formatterTagOn) {
145â†’                    CommentSuppressionHint(
146â†’                        this,
147â†’                        HashSet(parts.tailToRuleIds()),
148â†’                        BLOCK_END,
149â†’                    )
150â†’                } else {
151â†’                    null
152â†’                }
153â†’            }
154â†’
155â†’    private fun MutableList<CommentSuppressionHint>.toSuppressionHints(rootNode: ASTNode): MutableList<SuppressionHint> {
156â†’        val suppressionHints = mutableListOf<SuppressionHint>()
157â†’        val blockCommentSuppressionHints = mutableListOf<CommentSuppressionHint>()
158â†’        forEach { commentSuppressionHint ->
159â†’            when (commentSuppressionHint.type) {
160â†’                EOL -> {
161â†’                    val commentNode = commentSuppressionHint.node
162â†’                    suppressionHints.add(
163â†’                        SuppressionHint(
164â†’                            IntRange(commentNode.prevNewLineOffset(), commentNode.startOffset),
165â†’                            commentSuppressionHint.disabledRuleIds,
166â†’                        ),
167â†’                    )
168â†’                }
169â†’
170â†’                BLOCK_START -> {
171â†’                    blockCommentSuppressionHints.add(commentSuppressionHint)
172â†’                }
173â†’
174â†’                BLOCK_END -> {
175â†’                    // match open hint
176â†’                    Unit
177â†’                    blockCommentSuppressionHints
178â†’                        .lastOrNull { it.disabledRuleIds == commentSuppressionHint.disabledRuleIds }
179â†’                        ?.let { openHint ->
180â†’                            blockCommentSuppressionHints.remove(openHint)
181â†’                            suppressionHints.add(
182â†’                                SuppressionHint(
183â†’                                    IntRange(openHint.node.startOffset, commentSuppressionHint.node.startOffset - 1),
184â†’                                    commentSuppressionHint.disabledRuleIds,
185â†’                                ),
186â†’                            )
187â†’                        }
188â†’                }
189â†’            }
190â†’        }
191â†’        suppressionHints.addAll(
192â†’            blockCommentSuppressionHints.map {
193â†’                SuppressionHint(
194â†’                    IntRange(it.node.startOffset, rootNode.textLength),
195â†’                    it.disabledRuleIds,
196â†’                )
197â†’            },
198â†’        )
199â†’        return suppressionHints
200â†’    }
201â†’
202â†’    private fun ASTNode.prevNewLineOffset(): Int =
203â†’        prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
204â†’            ?.let { it.startOffset + it.text.lastIndexOf('\n') + 1 }
205â†’            ?: 0
206â†’
207â†’    private fun List<String>.tailToRuleIds() = tail().mapNotNull { createRuleIdOrNull(it) }
208â†’
209â†’    private fun <T> List<T>.tail() = this.subList(1, this.size)
210â†’
211â†’    /**
212â†’     * Creates [SuppressionHint] from annotations of given [PsiElement]. Returns null if no targetAnnotations are
213â†’     * present or no mapping exists between annotations' values and ktlint rules
214â†’     */
215â†’    private fun PsiElement.createSuppressionHintFromAnnotations(): SuppressionHint? =
216â†’        (this as? KtAnnotated)?.let { ktAnnotated ->
217â†’            ktAnnotated
218â†’                .annotationEntries
219â†’                .filter {
220â†’                    it.calleeExpression
221â†’                        ?.constructorReferenceExpression
222â†’                        ?.getReferencedName() in SUPPRESS_ANNOTATIONS
223â†’                }.flatMap(KtAnnotationEntry::getValueArguments)
224â†’                .mapNotNull { it.toRuleId(SUPPRESS_ANNOTATION_RULE_MAP) }
225â†’                .let { suppressedRuleIds ->
226â†’                    when {
227â†’                        suppressedRuleIds.isEmpty() -> null
228â†’                        suppressedRuleIds.contains(SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID) ->
229â†’                            SuppressionHint(
230â†’                                IntRange(ktAnnotated.startOffset, ktAnnotated.endOffset),
231â†’                                emptySet(),
232â†’                            )
233â†’
234â†’                        else ->
235â†’                            SuppressionHint(
236â†’                                IntRange(ktAnnotated.startOffset, ktAnnotated.endOffset),
237â†’                                suppressedRuleIds.toSet(),
238â†’                            )
239â†’                    }
240â†’                }
241â†’        }
242â†’
243â†’    private fun ValueArgument.toRuleId(annotationValueToRuleMapping: Map<String, RuleId>): RuleId? =
244â†’        getArgumentExpression()
245â†’            ?.text
246â†’            ?.removeSurrounding("\"")
247â†’            ?.let { argumentExpressionText ->
248â†’                when {
249â†’                    argumentExpressionText == "ktlint" -> {
250â†’                        // Disable all rules
251â†’                        SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID
252â†’                    }
253â†’                    argumentExpressionText.startsWith("ktlint:") -> {
254â†’                        // Disable specific rule
255â†’                        argumentExpressionText
256â†’                            .removePrefix("ktlint:")
257â†’                            .let { createRuleIdOrNull(it) }
258â†’                    }
259â†’                    else -> {
260â†’                        // Disable specific rule if the annotation value is mapped to a specific rule
261â†’                        annotationValueToRuleMapping[argumentExpressionText]
262â†’                    }
263â†’                }
264â†’            }
265â†’
266â†’    private fun createRuleIdOrNull(ruleId: String): RuleId? =
267â†’        try {
268â†’            // For backwards compatibility the suppression hints have to be prefixed with the standard rule set id when the rule id is
269â†’            // not prefixed with any rule set id.
270â†’            RuleId
271â†’                .prefixWithStandardRuleSetIdWhenMissing(ruleId)
272â†’                .let { RuleId(it) }
273â†’        } catch (illegalArgumentException: IllegalArgumentException) {
274â†’            // Ktlint should not terminate with an exception in case the code being scanned contains a suppression for a non-existing rule.
275â†’            // Instead, a warning should be printed and the invalid reference is to be ignored. The original ruleId is printed in the
276â†’            // warning message so that user will not go searching for the fully qualified rule id while the code actually contained an
277â†’            // unqualified ruleId.
278â†’            LOGGER.warn {
279â†’                """
280â†’                Can not suppress rule with id '$ruleId'. Please check and fix references to this rule in your code.
281â†’                    Underlying cause: ${illegalArgumentException.message}
282â†’                """.trimIndent()
283â†’            }
284â†’            null
285â†’        }
286â†’
287â†’    /**
288â†’     * @param range zero-based range of lines where lint errors should be suppressed
289â†’     * @param disabledRuleIds empty set means "all"
290â†’     */
291â†’    private data class SuppressionHint(
292â†’        val range: IntRange,
293â†’        val disabledRuleIds: Set<RuleId> = emptySet(),
294â†’    )
295â†’
296â†’    private data class CommentSuppressionHint(
297â†’        val node: ASTNode,
298â†’        val disabledRuleIds: Set<RuleId> = emptySet(),
299â†’        val type: Type,
300â†’    ) {
301â†’        enum class Type {
302â†’            EOL,
303â†’            BLOCK_START,
304â†’            BLOCK_END,
305â†’        }
306â†’    }
307â†’
308â†’    private const val KTLINT_DISABLE = "ktlint-disable"
309â†’    private const val KTLINT_ENABLE = "ktlint-enable"
310â†’}
311â†’

content.text: Now let me update the `NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule.kt` to remove the special handling for ktlint suppression directives:

conversation_history.content:
1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
 2â†’
 3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT
 4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EOL_COMMENT
 5â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 6â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule
 7â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.Mode.REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED
 8â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 9â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
10â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
11â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
12â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
13â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
14â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
15â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
16â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
17â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
18â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiCommentImpl
19â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
20â†’
21â†’/**
22â†’ * A block comment following another element on the same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
23â†’ */
24â†’public class NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule :
25â†’    StandardRule(
26â†’        id = "no-single-line-block-comment",
27â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
28â†’            setOf(
29â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
30â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
31â†’            ),
32â†’        visitorModifiers = setOf(RunAfterRule(COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED)),
33â†’    ),
34â†’    Rule.Experimental,
35â†’    Rule.OfficialCodeStyle {
36â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
37â†’        node: ASTNode,
38â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
39â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
40â†’    ) {
41â†’        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
42â†’            val afterBlockComment =
43â†’                node
44â†’                    .leaves()
45â†’                    .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.textContains('\n') }
46â†’                    .firstOrNull()
47â†’                    ?: node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
48â†’
49â†’            if (!node.textContains('\n') &&
50â†’                !node.isKtlintSuppressionDirective() &&
51â†’                afterBlockComment.nextLeaf().isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull()
52â†’            ) {
53â†’                emit(node.startOffset, "Replace the block comment with an EOL comment", true)
54â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
55â†’                    node.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
56â†’                }
57â†’            }
58â†’        }
59â†’    }
60â†’
61â†’    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
62â†’        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
63â†’        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
64â†’        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
65â†’        rawRemove()
66â†’    }
67â†’
68â†’    private fun ASTNode?.isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull() = this == null || this.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
69â†’
70â†’    // TODO: Remove when ktlint suppression directive in comments are no longer supported
71â†’    private fun ASTNode?.isKtlintSuppressionDirective() =
72â†’        this
73â†’            ?.text
74â†’            ?.removePrefix("/*")
75â†’            ?.removeSuffix("*/")
76â†’            ?.trim()
77â†’            ?.let { it.startsWith("ktlint-enable") || it.startsWith("ktlint-disable") }
78â†’            ?: false
79â†’}
80â†’
81â†’public val NO_SINGLE_LINE_BLOCK_COMMENT_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule().ruleId
82â†’

1â†’## Why should I use ktlint?
  2â†’
  3â†’the short answer is **Simplicity**.
  4â†’
  5â†’Spending time on configuration (and maintenance down the road) of hundred-line long style config file(s) is counter-productive. Instead of wasting your energy on something that has no business value - focus on what really matters (not debating whether to use tabs or spaces).
  6â†’
  7â†’By using ktlint you put the importance of code clarity and community conventions over personal preferences. This makes things easier for people reading your code as well as frees you from having to document and explain what style potential contributor(s) have to follow.
  8â†’
  9â†’ktlint is a single binary with both linter & formatter included. All you need is to drop it in (no need to get [overwhelmed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decision_fatigue) while choosing among [dozens of code style options](https://checkstyle.sourceforge.net/checks.html)).
 10â†’
 11â†’##  How do I enable or disable a rule?
 12â†’
 13â†’An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
 14â†’```editorconfig
 15â†’ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 16â†’ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 17â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 18â†’```
 19â†’
 20â†’!!! note
 21â†’    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
 22â†’
 23â†’##  How do I enable or disable a rule set?
 24â†’
 25â†’All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
 26â†’```editorconfig
 27â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 28â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
 29â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 30â†’```
 31â†’
 32â†’!!! note
 33â†’    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All `experimental` rules are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.
 34â†’
 35â†’## Can I have my own rules on top of ktlint?
 36â†’
 37â†’Absolutely, "no configuration" doesn't mean "no extensibility". You can add your own ruleset(s) to discover potential bugs, check for anti-patterns, etc.
 38â†’
 39â†’See [adding a custom rule set](../api/custom-rule-set/) for more information.
 40â†’
 41â†’## How do I suppress errors for a line/block/file?
 42â†’
 43â†’!!! tip
 44â†’    Suppressing a `ktlint` violation is meant primarily as an escape latch for the rare cases when **ktlint** is not able to produce the correct result. Please report any such instances using [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues)).
 45â†’
 46â†’To disable a specific rule you'll need the rule identifier which is displayed at the end of the lint error.
 47â†’
 48â†’An error can be suppressed using:
 49â†’
 50â†’* EOL comments
 51â†’* Block comments
 52â†’* @Suppress annotations
 53â†’
 54â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Suppress annotation"
 55â†’
 56â†’    ```kotlin
 57â†’    // Suppressing all rules for the entire file
 58â†’    @file:Suppress("ktlint")
 59â†’
 60â†’    // Suppress a single rule (with id 'rule-id', defined in rule set with id 'rule-set-id') from the annotated construct
 61â†’    @Suppress("ktlint:rule-set-id:rule-id")
 62â†’    class Foo {}
 63â†’
 64â†’    // Suppress multiple rules for the annotated construct
 65â†’    @Suppress("ktlint:standard:no-wildcard-imports", "ktlint:custom-rule-set-id:custom-rule-id")
 66â†’    import foo.*
 67â†’
 68â†’    // Suppress all rules for the annotated construct
 69â†’    @Suppress("ktlint")
 70â†’    import foo.*
 71â†’    ```
 72â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) EOL comments"
 73â†’
 74â†’    ```kotlin
 75â†’    // Suppress a single rule for the commented line
 76â†’    import foo.* // ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports
 77â†’
 78â†’    // Suppress multiple rules for the commented line
 79â†’    import foo.* // ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_other-rule-id
 80â†’
 81â†’    // Suppress all rules for the commented line
 82â†’    import foo.* // ktlint-disable
 83â†’    ```
 84â†’
 85â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Block comments"
 86â†’
 87â†’    ```kotlin
 88â†’    // Suppress a single rule for all code between the start and end tag
 89â†’    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 90â†’    import foo.*
 91â†’    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 92â†’
 93â†’    // Suppress multiple rules for all code between the start and end tag
 94â†’    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 95â†’    import foo.*
 96â†’    /* ktlint-enable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 97â†’
 98â†’    // Suppress all rules for all code between the start and end tag
 99â†’    /* ktlint-disable */
100â†’    import foo.*
101â†’    /* ktlint-enable */
102â†’    ```
103â†’
104â†’!!! important
105â†’    When using the block comments, the `ktlint-enable` directive needs to specify the exact same rule-id's and in the same order as the `ktlint-disable` directive.
106â†’
107â†’!!! warning
108â†’    From a consistency perspective seen, it might be best to **not** mix the (EOL/Block) comment style with the annotation style in the same project.
109â†’
110â†’## How do I globally disable a rule without `.editorconfig`?
111â†’
112â†’When using Ktlint CLI, you may pass a list of disabled rules via the `--disabled_rules` command line flag. The value is a comma separated list of rule id's that have to be disabled. The rule id must be fully qualified (e.g. must be prefixed with the rule set id). 
113â†’
114â†’
115â†’## Why is `.editorconfig` property `disabled_rules` deprecated and how do I resolve this?
116â†’
117â†’The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are deprecated as of KtLint version `0.48` and are removed in version `0.49`. Those properties contain a comma separated list of rules which are disabled. Using a comma separated list of values has some disadvantages.
118â†’
119â†’A big disadvantage is that it is not possible to override the property partially in an `.editorconfig` file in a subpackage. Another disadvantage is that it is not possible to express explicitly that a rule is enabled. Lastly, (qualified) rule ids can be 20 characters or longer, which makes a list with multiple entries hard to read.
120â†’
121â†’Starting with KtLint `0.48` entire rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set). Examples:
122â†’```editorconfig
123â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
124â†’ktlint_standard_final-newline = enabled # Enables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
125â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
126â†’ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = disabled # Disables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
127â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
128â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
129â†’```
130â†’
131â†’!!! note
132â†’    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All `experimental` rules are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.
133â†’
134â†’!!! note
135â†’    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.  
136â†’
137â†’## Why is wildcard import `java.util.*` not reported by the `no-wildcard-imports` rule?
138â†’
139â†’The `no-wildcard-imports` rule forbids wildcard imports, except for imports defined in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`. If this property is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior.
140â†’
141â†’## Can a new toggle be added to optionally (enable/disable) format code in a particular way?
142â†’
143â†’Ktlint can be configured by enabling and disabling rules. Some rules can be configured in more details with additional `.editorconfig` properties. Regularly, a new configuration option is requested to modify behavior in existing rules.
144â†’
145â†’Ktlint is restrictive with adding additional configuration settings to customize behavior in rules. Each configuration option that Ktlint offers comes with complexity that has to be maintained by only a couple of maintainers. As of that, we cannot provide tens or even hundreds of such options.
146â†’
147â†’Less configuration options also means less discussions in teams about settings to use. Unfortunately this means that you cannot tweak Ktlint exactly to the format you prefer.
148â†’
149â†’!!! tip
150â†’    Any idea for a new configuration option is valuable. Please create an issue for it so that it can be considered to incorporate it in Ktlint.
151â†’
152â†’## Can I use KtLint to directly format the code I'm generating with KotlinPoet?
153â†’
154â†’Yes, it is possible to use KtLint to directly format the code generated with KotlinPoet. 
155â†’To do so, you must include the dependencies `com.pinterest.ktlint:ktlint-core` and `com.pinterest.ktlint:ktlint-ruleset-standard` in your Gradle/Maven project.
156â†’
157â†’!!! warning
158â†’    Do not include the dependency `com.pinterest:ktlint` as that would import the entire ktlint project including unwanted dependencies. Besides a much bigger artifact, it might also result in problems regarding logging.
159â†’
160â†’To format the output of KotlinPoet with KtLint, you can use the following snippet:
161â†’
162â†’```kotlin
163â†’val ruleProviders = buildSet {
164â†’  ServiceLoader
165â†’      .load(RuleSetProviderV2::class.java)
166â†’      .flatMapTo(this) { it.getRuleProviders() }
167â†’}
168â†’val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
169â†’  ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
170â†’  editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults.load(EDITORCONFIG_PATH),
171â†’)
172â†’ktLintRuleEngine.format(outputDir.toPath())
173â†’```
174â†’Here, outputDir refers to the directory of the generated files by KotlinPoet, ktLintRuleEngine is an instance of KtLint rule engine.
175â†’
176â†’It is also possible to format file-by-file the output of KotlinPoet if you write your `FileSpec` to a `StringBuilder()`, instead of a `File`, 
177â†’and send the generated code as `String` to KtLint inside a `CodeSnippet`:
178â†’```kotlin
179â†’kotlinFile.writeText(
180â†’  ktLintRuleEngine.format(
181â†’    Code.CodeSnippet(
182â†’      stringBuilder.toString()
183â†’    )
184â†’  )
185â†’)
186â†’```
187â†’
188â†’# Are formatter tags respected?
189â†’
190â†’As of version `0.49.x` the formatter tags of IntelliJ IDEA are respected. By default, those formatter tags are disabled. The formatter tags can be enabled with `.editorconfig` properties below:
191â†’```editorconfig
192â†’ij_formatter_tags_enabled = true # Defaults to 'false'
193â†’ij_formatter_off_tag = some-custom-off-tag # Defaults to '@formatter:off'
194â†’ij_formatter_on_tag = some-custom-on-tag # Defaults to '@formatter:on'
195â†’```
196â†’
197â†’When enabled, the ktlint rule checking is disabled for all code surrounded by the formatter tags.
198â†’

{"file_path": "/project/documentation/snapshot/docs/faq.md", "content": "## Why should I use ktlint?

the short answer is **Simplicity**.

Spending time on configuration (and maintenance down the road) of hundred-line long style config file(s) is counter-productive. Instead of wasting your energy on something that has no business value - focus on what really matters (not debating whether to use tabs or spaces).

By using ktlint you put the importance of code clarity and community conventions over personal preferences. This makes things easier for people reading your code as well as frees you from having to document and explain what style potential contributor(s) have to follow.

ktlint is a single binary with both linter & formatter included. All you need is to drop it in (no need to get [overwhelmed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decision_fatigue) while choosing among [dozens of code style options](https://checkstyle.sourceforge.net/checks.html)).

##  How do I enable or disable a rule?

An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
```editorconfig
ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
```

!!! note
    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.

##  How do I enable or disable a rule set?

All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
```editorconfig
ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
```

!!! note
    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All `experimental` rules are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.

## Can I have my own rules on top of ktlint?

Absolutely, "no configuration" doesn't mean "no extensibility". You can add your own ruleset(s) to discover potential bugs, check for anti-patterns, etc.

See [adding a custom rule set](../api/custom-rule-set/) for more information.

## How do I suppress errors for a line/block/file?

!!! tip
    Suppressing a `ktlint` violation is meant primarily as an escape latch for the rare cases when **ktlint** is not able to produce the correct result. Please report any such instances using [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues)).

To disable a specific rule you'll need the rule identifier which is displayed at the end of the lint error.

An error can be suppressed using the `@Suppress` annotation:

```kotlin
// Suppressing all rules for the entire file
@file:Suppress("ktlint")

// Suppress a single rule (with id 'rule-id', defined in rule set with id 'rule-set-id') from the annotated construct
@Suppress("ktlint:rule-set-id:rule-id")
class Foo {}

// Suppress multiple rules for the annotated construct
@Suppress("ktlint:standard:no-wildcard-imports", "ktlint:custom-rule-set-id:custom-rule-id")
import foo.*

// Suppress all rules for the annotated construct
@Suppress("ktlint")
import foo.*
```

## How do I globally disable a rule without `.editorconfig`?

When using Ktlint CLI, you may pass a list of disabled rules via the `--disabled_rules` command line flag. The value is a comma separated list of rule id's that have to be disabled. The rule id must be fully qualified (e.g. must be prefixed with the rule set id). 


## Why is `.editorconfig` property `disabled_rules` deprecated and how do I resolve this?

The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are deprecated as of KtLint version `0.48` and are removed in version `0.49`. Those properties contain a comma separated list of rules which are disabled. Using a comma separated list of values has some disadvantages.

A big disadvantage is that it is not possible to override the property partially in an `.editorconfig` file in a subpackage. Another disadvantage is that it is not possible to express explicitly that a rule is enabled. Lastly, (qualified) rule ids can be 20 characters or longer, which makes a list with multiple entries hard to read.

Starting with KtLint `0.48` entire rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set). Examples:
```editorconfig
ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
ktlint_standard_final-newline = enabled # Enables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = disabled # Disables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
```

!!! note
    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All `experimental` rules are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.

!!! note
    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.  

## Why is wildcard import `java.util.*` not reported by the `no-wildcard-imports` rule?

The `no-wildcard-imports` rule forbids wildcard imports, except for imports defined in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`. If this property is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior.

## Can a new toggle be added to optionally (enable/disable) format code in a particular way?

Ktlint can be configured by enabling and disabling rules. Some rules can be configured in more details with additional `.editorconfig` properties. Regularly, a new configuration option is requested to modify behavior in existing rules.

Ktlint is restrictive with adding additional configuration settings to customize behavior in rules. Each configuration option that Ktlint offers comes with complexity that has to be maintained by only a couple of maintainers. As of that, we cannot provide tens or even hundreds of such options.

Less configuration options also means less discussions in teams about settings to use. Unfortunately this means that you cannot tweak Ktlint exactly to the format you prefer.

!!! tip
    Any idea for a new configuration option is valuable. Please create an issue for it so that it can be considered to incorporate it in Ktlint.

## Can I use KtLint to directly format the code I'm generating with KotlinPoet?

Yes, it is possible to use KtLint to directly format the code generated with KotlinPoet. 
To do so, you must include the dependencies `com.pinterest.ktlint:ktlint-core` and `com.pinterest.ktlint:ktlint-ruleset-standard` in your Gradle/Maven project.

!!! warning
    Do not include the dependency `com.pinterest:ktlint` as that would import the entire ktlint project including unwanted dependencies. Besides a much bigger artifact, it might also result in problems regarding logging.

To format the output of KotlinPoet with KtLint, you can use the following snippet:

```kotlin
val ruleProviders = buildSet {
  ServiceLoader
      .load(RuleSetProviderV2::class.java)
      .flatMapTo(this) { it.getRuleProviders() }
}
val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
  ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
  editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults.load(EDITORCONFIG_PATH),
)
ktLintRuleEngine.format(outputDir.toPath())
```
Here, outputDir refers to the directory of the generated files by KotlinPoet, ktLintRuleEngine is an instance of KtLint rule engine.

It is also possible to format file-by-file the output of KotlinPoet if you write your `FileSpec` to a `StringBuilder()`, instead of a `File`, 
and send the generated code as `String` to KtLint inside a `CodeSnippet`:
```kotlin
kotlinFile.writeText(
  ktLintRuleEngine.format(
    Code.CodeSnippet(
      stringBuilder.toString()
    )
  )
)
```

# Are formatter tags respected?

As of version `0.49.x` the formatter tags of IntelliJ IDEA are respected. By default, those formatter tags are disabled. The formatter tags can be enabled with `.editorconfig` properties below:
```editorconfig
ij_formatter_tags_enabled = true # Defaults to 'false'
ij_formatter_off_tag = some-custom-off-tag # Defaults to '@formatter:off'
ij_formatter_on_tag = some-custom-on-tag # Defaults to '@formatter:on'
```

When enabled, the ktlint rule checking is disabled for all code surrounded by the formatter tags.
"}

 22â†’/**
 23â†’ * Detects if given `ruleId` at given `offset` is suppressed.
 24â†’ */
 25â†’internal typealias SuppressionLocator = (offset: Int, ruleId: RuleId) -> Boolean
 26â†’
 27â†’internal object SuppressionLocatorBuilder {
 28â†’    /**
 29â†’     * No suppression is detected. Always returns `false`.
 30â†’     */
 31â†’    private val NO_SUPPRESSION: SuppressionLocator = { _, _ -> false }
 32â†’
 33â†’    private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 34â†’
 35â†’    /**
 36â†’     * Mapping of non-ktlint annotations to ktlint-annotation so that ktlint rules will be suppressed automatically
 37â†’     * when specific non-ktlint annotations are found. The prevents that developers have to specify multiple annotations
 38â†’     * for the same violation.
 39â†’     */
 40â†’    private val SUPPRESS_ANNOTATION_RULE_MAP =
 41â†’        mapOf(
 42â†’            // It would have been nice if the official rule id's as defined in the Rules themselves could have been used here. But that would
 43â†’            // introduce a circular dependency between the ktlint-rule-engine and the ktlint-ruleset-standard modules.
 44â†’            "EnumEntryName" to RuleId("standard:enum-entry-name-case"),
 45â†’            "RemoveCurlyBracesFromTemplate" to RuleId("standard:string-template"),
 46â†’            "ClassName" to RuleId("standard:class-naming"),
 47â†’            "FunctionName" to RuleId("standard:function-naming"),
 48â†’            "PackageName" to RuleId("standard:package-name"),
 49â†’            "PropertyName" to RuleId("standard:property-naming"),
 50â†’        )
 51â†’    private val SUPPRESS_ANNOTATIONS = setOf("Suppress", "SuppressWarnings")
 52â†’    private val SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID = RuleId("ktlint:suppress-all-rules")
 53â†’
 54â†’    /**
 55â†’     * Builds [SuppressionLocator] for given [rootNode] of AST tree.
 56â†’     */
 57â†’    fun buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(
 58â†’        rootNode: ASTNode,
 59â†’        editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 60â†’    ): SuppressionLocator {
 61â†’        val hintsList = collect(rootNode, FormatterTags.from(editorConfig))
 62â†’        return if (hintsList.isEmpty()) {
 63â†’            NO_SUPPRESSION
 64â†’        } else {
 65â†’            toSuppressedRegionsLocator(hintsList)
 66â†’        }
 67â†’    }
 68â†’
 69â†’    private fun toSuppressedRegionsLocator(hintsList: List<SuppressionHint>): SuppressionLocator =
 70â†’        { offset, ruleId ->
 71â†’            hintsList
 72â†’                .filter { offset in it.range }
 73â†’                .any { hint -> hint.disabledRuleIds.isEmpty() || hint.disabledRuleIds.contains(ruleId) }
 74â†’        }
 75â†’
 76â†’    private fun collect(
 77â†’        rootNode: ASTNode,
 78â†’        formatterTags: FormatterTags,
 79â†’    ): List<SuppressionHint> {
 80â†’        val suppressionHints = ArrayList<SuppressionHint>()
 81â†’        val commentSuppressionsHints = mutableListOf<CommentSuppressionHint>()
 82â†’        rootNode.collect { node ->
 83â†’            node
 84â†’                .takeIf { it is PsiComment }
 85â†’                ?.createSuppressionHintFromComment(formatterTags)
 86â†’                ?.let { commentSuppressionsHints.add(it) }
 87â†’
 88â†’            // Extract all Suppress annotations and create SuppressionHints
 89â†’            node
 90â†’                .psi
 91â†’                .safeAs<KtAnnotated>()
 92â†’                ?.createSuppressionHintFromAnnotations()
 93â†’                ?.let { suppressionHints.add(it) }
 94â†’        }
 95â†’
 96â†’        return suppressionHints.plus(
 97â†’            commentSuppressionsHints.toSuppressionHints(rootNode),
 98â†’        )
 99â†’    }
100â†’
101â†’    private fun ASTNode.collect(block: (node: ASTNode) -> Unit) {
102â†’        block(this)
103â†’        this
104â†’            .getChildren(null)
105â†’            .forEach { it.collect(block) }
106â†’    }
107â†’
108â†’    private fun ASTNode.createSuppressionHintFromComment(formatterTags: FormatterTags): CommentSuppressionHint? =
109â†’        if (text.startsWith("//")) {
110â†’            createSuppressionHintFromEolComment(formatterTags)
111â†’        } else {
112â†’            createSuppressionHintFromBlockComment(formatterTags)
113â†’        }
114â†’
115â†’    private fun ASTNode.createSuppressionHintFromEolComment(formatterTags: FormatterTags): CommentSuppressionHint? =
116â†’        text
117â†’            .removePrefix("//")
118â†’            .trim()
119â†’            .split(" ")
120â†’            .takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
121â†’            ?.takeIf { it[0] == KTLINT_DISABLE || it[0] == formatterTags.formatterTagOff }
122â†’            ?.let { parts ->
123â†’                CommentSuppressionHint(
124â†’                    this,
125â†’                    HashSet(parts.tailToRuleIds()),
126â†’                    EOL,
127â†’                )
128â†’            }
129â†’
130â†’    private fun ASTNode.createSuppressionHintFromBlockComment(formatterTags: FormatterTags): CommentSuppressionHint? =
131â†’        text
132â†’            .removePrefix("/*")
133â†’            .removeSuffix("*/")
134â†’            .trim()
135â†’            .split(" ")
136â†’            .takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
137â†’            ?.let { parts ->
138â†’                if (parts[0] == KTLINT_DISABLE || parts[0] == formatterTags.formatterTagOff) {
139â†’                    CommentSuppressionHint(
140â†’                        this,
141â†’                        HashSet(parts.tailToRuleIds()),
142â†’                        BLOCK_START,
143â†’                    )
144â†’                } else if (parts[0] == KTLINT_ENABLE || parts[0] == formatterTags.formatterTagOn) {
145â†’                    CommentSuppressionHint(
146â†’                        this,
147â†’                        HashSet(parts.tailToRuleIds()),
148â†’                        BLOCK_END,
149â†’                    )
150â†’                } else {
151â†’                    null
152â†’                }
153â†’            }
154â†’
155â†’    private fun MutableList<CommentSuppressionHint>.toSuppressionHints(rootNode: ASTNode): MutableList<SuppressionHint> {
156â†’        val suppressionHints = mutableListOf<SuppressionHint>()
157â†’        val blockCommentSuppressionHints = mutableListOf<CommentSuppressionHint>()
158â†’        forEach { commentSuppressionHint ->
159â†’            when (commentSuppressionHint.type) {
160â†’                EOL -> {
161â†’                    val commentNode = commentSuppressionHint.node
162â†’                    suppressionHints.add(
163â†’                        SuppressionHint(
164â†’                            IntRange(commentNode.prevNewLineOffset(), commentNode.startOffset),
165â†’                            commentSuppressionHint.disabledRuleIds,
166â†’                        ),
167â†’                    )
168â†’                }
169â†’
170â†’                BLOCK_START -> {
171â†’                    blockCommentSuppressionHints.add(commentSuppressionHint)
172â†’                }
173â†’
174â†’                BLOCK_END -> {
175â†’                    // match open hint
176â†’                    Unit
177â†’                    blockCommentSuppressionHints
178â†’                        .lastOrNull { it.disabledRuleIds == commentSuppressionHint.disabledRuleIds }
179â†’                        ?.let { openHint ->
180â†’                            blockCommentSuppressionHints.remove(openHint)
181â†’                            suppressionHints.add(
182â†’                                SuppressionHint(
183â†’                                    IntRange(openHint.node.startOffset, commentSuppressionHint.node.startOffset - 1),
184â†’                                    commentSuppressionHint.disabledRuleIds,
185â†’                                ),
186â†’                            )
187â†’                        }
188â†’                }
189â†’            }
190â†’        }
191â†’        suppressionHints.addAll(
192â†’            blockCommentSuppressionHints.map {
193â†’                SuppressionHint(
194â†’                    IntRange(it.node.startOffset, rootNode.textLength),
195â†’                    it.disabledRuleIds,
196â†’                )
197â†’            },
198â†’        )
199â†’        return suppressionHints
200â†’    }

202â†’    private fun ASTNode.prevNewLineOffset(): Int =
203â†’        prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
204â†’            ?.let { it.startOffset + it.text.lastIndexOf('\n') + 1 }
205â†’            ?: 0

207â†’    private fun List<String>.tailToRuleIds() = tail().mapNotNull { createRuleIdOrNull(it) }
208â†’
209â†’    private fun <T> List<T>.tail() = this.subList(1, this.size)

211â†’    /**
212â†’     * Creates [SuppressionHint] from annotations of given [PsiElement]. Returns null if no targetAnnotations are
213â†’     * present or no mapping exists between annotations' values and ktlint rules
214â†’     */
215â†’    private fun PsiElement.createSuppressionHintFromAnnotations(): SuppressionHint? =
216â†’        (this as? KtAnnotated)?.let { ktAnnotated ->
217â†’            ktAnnotated
218â†’                .annotationEntries
219â†’                .filter {
220â†’                    it.calleeExpression
221â†’                        ?.constructorReferenceExpression
222â†’                        ?.getReferencedName() in SUPPRESS_ANNOTATIONS
223â†’                }.flatMap(KtAnnotationEntry::getValueArguments)
224â†’                .mapNotNull { it.toRuleId(SUPPRESS_ANNOTATION_RULE_MAP) }
225â†’                .let { suppressedRuleIds ->
226â†’                    when {
227â†’                        suppressedRuleIds.isEmpty() -> null
228â†’                        suppressedRuleIds.contains(SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID) ->
229â†’                            SuppressionHint(
230â†’                                IntRange(ktAnnotated.startOffset, ktAnnotated.endOffset),
231â†’                                emptySet(),
232â†’                            )
233â†’
234â†’                        else ->
235â†’                            SuppressionHint(
236â†’                                IntRange(ktAnnotated.startOffset, ktAnnotated.endOffset),
237â†’                                suppressedRuleIds.toSet(),
238â†’                            )
239â†’                    }
240â†’                }
241â†’        }
242â†’
243â†’    private fun ValueArgument.toRuleId(annotationValueToRuleMapping: Map<String, RuleId>): RuleId? =
244â†’        getArgumentExpression()
245â†’            ?.text
246â†’            ?.removeSurrounding("\"")
247â†’            ?.let { argumentExpressionText ->
248â†’                when {
249â†’                    argumentExpressionText == "ktlint" -> {
250â†’                        // Disable all rules
251â†’                        SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID
252â†’                    }
253â†’                    argumentExpressionText.startsWith("ktlint:") -> {
254â†’                        // Disable specific rule
255â†’                        argumentExpressionText
256â†’                            .removePrefix("ktlint:")
257â†’                            .let { createRuleIdOrNull(it) }
258â†’                    }
259â†’                    else -> {
260â†’                        // Disable specific rule if the annotation value is mapped to a specific rule
261â†’                        annotationValueToRuleMapping[argumentExpressionText]
262â†’                    }
263â†’                }
264â†’            }
265â†’
266â†’    private fun createRuleIdOrNull(ruleId: String): RuleId? =
267â†’        try {
268â†’            // For backwards compatibility the suppression hints have to be prefixed with the standard rule set id when the rule id is
269â†’            // not prefixed with any rule set id.
270â†’            RuleId
271â†’                .prefixWithStandardRuleSetIdWhenMissing(ruleId)
272â†’                .let { RuleId(it) }
273â†’        } catch (illegalArgumentException: IllegalArgumentException) {
274â†’            // Ktlint should not terminate with an exception in case the code being scanned contains a suppression for a non-existing rule.
275â†’            // Instead, a warning should be printed and the invalid reference is to be ignored. The original ruleId is printed in the
276â†’            // warning message so that user will not go searching for the fully qualified rule id while the code actually contained an
277â†’            // unqualified ruleId.
278â†’            LOGGER.warn {
279â†’                """
280â†’                Can not suppress rule with id '$ruleId'. Please check and fix references to this rule in your code.
281â†’                    Underlying cause: ${illegalArgumentException.message}
282â†’                """.trimIndent()
283â†’            }
284â†’            null
285â†’        }

 29â†’internal class RuleExecutionContext private constructor(
 30â†’    val code: Code,
 31â†’    val rootNode: FileASTNode,
 32â†’    val ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
 33â†’    val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 34â†’    val positionInTextLocator: (offset: Int) -> LineAndColumn,
 35â†’) {
 36â†’    private lateinit var suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator
 37â†’
 38â†’    init {
 39â†’        rebuildSuppressionLocator()
 40â†’    }
 41â†’
 42â†’    fun rebuildSuppressionLocator() {
 43â†’        suppressionLocator = SuppressionLocatorBuilder.buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(rootNode, editorConfig)
 44â†’    }
 45â†’
 46â†’    fun executeRule(
 47â†’        rule: Rule,
 48â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 49â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 50â†’    ) {
 51â†’        try {
 52â†’            rule.startTraversalOfAST()
 53â†’            rule.beforeFirstNode(
 54â†’                // The rule get access to an EditConfig which is filtered by the properties which are actually registered as being used by
 55â†’                // the rule. In this way it can be forced that the rule actually registers the properties that it uses and the field becomes
 56â†’                // reliable to be used by for example the ".editorconfig" file generator.
 57â†’                // Note that also the CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY is provided because that property is needed to determine the default value of an
 58â†’                // EditorConfigProperty that is not explicitly defined.
 59â†’                editorConfig.filterBy(rule.usesEditorConfigProperties.plus(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY)),
 60â†’            )
 61â†’            this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(rootNode, rule, autoCorrect, emit)
 62â†’            rule.afterLastNode()
 63â†’        } catch (e: RuleExecutionException) {
 64â†’            throw KtLintRuleException(
 65â†’                e.line,
 66â†’                e.col,
 67â†’                e.rule.ruleId.value,
 68â†’                """
 69â†’                Rule '${e.rule.ruleId.value}' throws exception in file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}' at position (${e.line}:${e.col})
 70â†’                   Rule maintainer: ${e.rule.about.maintainer}
 71â†’                   Issue tracker  : ${e.rule.about.issueTrackerUrl}
 72â†’                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
 73â†’                """.trimIndent(),
 74â†’                e.cause,
 75â†’            )
 76â†’        }
 77â†’    }
 78â†’
 79â†’    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 80â†’        node: ASTNode,
 81â†’        rule: Rule,
 82â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 83â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84â†’    ) {
 85â†’        /**
 86â†’         * The [suppressionLocator] can be changed during each visit of node when running [KtLintRuleEngine.format]. So a new handler is to
 87â†’         * be built before visiting the nodes.
 88â†’         */
 89â†’        val suppressHandler = SuppressHandler(suppressionLocator, autoCorrect, emit)
 90â†’        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 91â†’            try {
 92â†’                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 93â†’                    rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94â†’                }
 95â†’                if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 96â†’                    node
 97â†’                        .getChildren(null)
 98â†’                        .forEach { childNode ->
 99â†’                            suppressHandler.handle(childNode, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
100â†’                                this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
101â†’                                    childNode,
102â†’                                    rule,
103â†’                                    autoCorrect,
104â†’                                    emit,
105â†’                                )
106â†’                            }
107â†’                        }
108â†’                }
109â†’                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
110â†’                    rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
111â†’                }
112â†’            } catch (e: Exception) {
113â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
114â†’                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the
115â†’                    // original AST
116â†’                    throw RuleExecutionException(
117â†’                        rule,
118â†’                        0,
119â†’                        0,
120â†’                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
121â†’                        e.cause ?: e,
122â†’                    )
123â†’                } else {
124â†’                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(node.startOffset)
125â†’                    throw RuleExecutionException(
126â†’                        rule,
127â†’                        line,
128â†’                        col,
129â†’                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
130â†’                        e.cause ?: e,
131â†’                    )
132â†’                }
133â†’            }
134â†’        }
135â†’    }
136â†’
137â†’    companion object {
138â†’        internal fun createRuleExecutionContext(
139â†’            ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
140â†’            code: Code,
141â†’        ): RuleExecutionContext {
142â†’            val psiFileFactory = KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER.getKotlinPsiFileFactory(ktLintRuleEngine)
143â†’            val normalizedText = normalizeText(code.content)
144â†’            val positionInTextLocator = buildPositionInTextLocator(normalizedText)
145â†’
146â†’            val psiFileName =
147â†’                code
148â†’                    .filePath
149â†’                    ?.pathString
150â†’                    ?: if (code.script) {
151â†’                        "File.kts"
152â†’                    } else {
153â†’                        "File.kt"
154â†’                    }
155â†’            val psiFile =
156â†’                psiFileFactory.createFileFromText(
157â†’                    psiFileName,
158â†’                    KotlinLanguage.INSTANCE,
159â†’                    normalizedText,
160â†’                ) as KtFile
161â†’            psiFile
162â†’                .findErrorElement()
163â†’                ?.let { errorElement ->
164â†’                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(errorElement.textOffset)
165â†’                    throw KtLintParseException(line, col, errorElement.errorDescription)
166â†’                }
167â†’
168â†’            val rootNode = psiFile.node
169â†’
170â†’            val editorConfig =
171â†’                ktLintRuleEngine
172â†’                    .editorConfigLoader
173â†’                    .load(code.filePath)
174â†’                    .also {
175â†’                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
176â†’                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("disabled_rules", "0.49")
177â†’                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
178â†’                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("ktlint_disabled_rules", "0.49")
179â†’                    }
180â†’
181â†’            val ruleProviders =
182â†’                ktLintRuleEngine
183â†’                    .ruleProviders(
184â†’                        RuleExecutionRuleFilter(editorConfig),
185â†’                        RunAfterRuleFilter(),
186â†’                    )
187â†’
188â†’            return RuleExecutionContext(
189â†’                code,
190â†’                rootNode,
191â†’                ruleProviders,
192â†’                editorConfig,
193â†’                positionInTextLocator,
194â†’            )
195â†’        }
196â†’
197â†’        private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
198â†’            return text
199â†’                .replace("\r\n", "\n")
200â†’                .replace("\r", "\n")
201â†’                .replaceFirst(UTF8_BOM, "")
202â†’        }
203â†’
204â†’        private fun PsiElement.findErrorElement(): PsiErrorElement? {
205â†’            if (this is PsiErrorElement) {
206â†’                return this
207â†’            }
208â†’            this.children.forEach { child ->
209â†’                val errorElement = child.findErrorElement()
210â†’                if (errorElement != null) {
211â†’                    return errorElement
212â†’                }
213â†’            }
214â†’            return null
215â†’        }
216â†’    }
217â†’}
218â†’
219â†’private fun EditorConfig.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete(
220â†’    propertyName: String,
221â†’    ktlintVersion: String,
222â†’) {
223â†’    if (this.contains(propertyName)) {
224â†’        LOGGER.warn {
225â†’            "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' is obsolete and is not used by KtLint starting from version $ktlintVersion. Remove " +
226â†’                "the property from all '.editorconfig' files."
227â†’        }
228â†’    }
229â†’}

231â†’private class RuleExecutionException(
232â†’    val rule: Rule,
233â†’    val line: Int,
234â†’    val col: Int,
235â†’    override val cause: Throwable,
236â†’) : Throwable(cause)

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter
 2â†’
 3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine
 4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider
 5â†’
 6â†’/**
 7â†’ * Gets the rule provider for the [KtLintRuleEngine] by applying the [ruleFilters] in the given order on the set of [RuleProvider]s provided
 8â†’ * by the previous (or the initial list of [RuleProvider]s).
 9â†’ */
10â†’internal fun KtLintRuleEngine.ruleProviders(vararg ruleFilters: RuleFilter): Set<RuleProvider> {
11â†’    var ruleProviders = initialRuleProviders()
12â†’    val ruleFilterIterator = ruleFilters.iterator()
13â†’    while (ruleFilterIterator.hasNext()) {
14â†’        val ruleFilter = ruleFilterIterator.next()
15â†’        ruleProviders = ruleFilter.filter(ruleProviders)
16â†’    }
17â†’    return ruleProviders
18â†’}
19â†’
20â†’private fun KtLintRuleEngine.initialRuleProviders() =
21â†’    ruleProviders
22â†’        .distinctBy { it.ruleId }
23â†’        .toSet()
24â†’
25â†’internal interface RuleFilter {
26â†’    fun filter(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): Set<RuleProvider>
27â†’}

 36â†’public class KtLintRuleEngine(
 37â†’    /**
 38â†’     * The set of [RuleProvider]s to be invoked by the [KtLintRuleEngine]. A [RuleProvider] is able to create a new instance of a [Rule] so
 39â†’     * that it can keep internal state and be called thread-safe manner
 40â†’     */
 41â†’    public val ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
 42â†’    /**
 43â†’     * The default values for `.editorconfig` properties which are not set explicitly in any '.editorconfig' file located on the path of the
 44â†’     * file which is processed with the [KtLintRuleEngine]. If a property is set in [editorConfigDefaults] this takes precedence above the
 45â†’     * default values defined in the KtLint project.
 46â†’     */
 47â†’    public val editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS,
 48â†’    /**
 49â†’     * Override values for `.editorconfig` properties. If a property is set in [editorConfigOverride] it takes precedence above the same
 50â†’     * property being set in any other way.
 51â†’     */
 52â†’    public val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
 53â†’    /**
 54â†’     * Temporary flag to indicate that kotlin embeddable compiler is to be executed with (default) or without the extension point
 55â†’     * 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler'. This extension point is not (yet) supported in the preview of Kotlin 1.9. Some
 56â†’     * rules might no longer work and throw exceptions at runtime.
 57â†’     * It is unclear whether the extension point will be supported. Disabling this flag on the Kotlin 1.8 compiler has the same effect. As
 58â†’     * of that it can be used to assess the impact, and to fix rules before release of the Ktlint version which will be based on Kotlin 1.9.
 59â†’     */
 60â†’    public val enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint: Boolean = true,
 61â†’    /**
 62â†’     * **For internal use only**: indicates that linting was invoked from KtLint CLI tool. It enables some internals workarounds for Kotlin
 63â†’     * Compiler initialization. This property is likely to be removed in any of next versions without further notice.
 64â†’     */
 65â†’    public val isInvokedFromCli: Boolean = false,
 66â†’    /**
 67â†’     * The [FileSystem] to be used. This property is primarily intended to be used in unit tests. By specifying an alternative [FileSystem]
 68â†’     * the unit test gains control on whether the [EditorConfigLoader] should or should not read specific ".editorconfig" files. For
 69â†’     * example, it is considered unwanted that a unit test is influenced by the ".editorconfig" file of the project in which the unit test
 70â†’     * is included.
 71â†’     */
 72â†’    public val fileSystem: FileSystem = FileSystems.getDefault(),
 73â†’) {
 74â†’    init {

 76â†’            "A non-empty set of 'ruleProviders' need to be provided"
 77â†’        }
 78â†’    }
 79â†’
 80â†’    internal val editorConfigLoaderEc4j = EditorConfigLoaderEc4j(ruleProviders.propertyTypes())
 81â†’
 82â†’    internal val editorConfigLoader =
 83â†’        EditorConfigLoader(
 84â†’            fileSystem,
 85â†’            editorConfigLoaderEc4j,
 86â†’            editorConfigDefaults,
 87â†’            editorConfigOverride,
 88â†’        )
 89â†’
 90â†’    /**
 91â†’     * Check the [code] for lint errors. If [code] is path as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on the path to file are taken
 92â†’     * into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
 93â†’     *
 94â†’     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
 95â†’     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
 96â†’     */
 97â†’    public fun lint(
 98â†’        code: Code,
 99â†’        callback: (LintError) -> Unit = { },
100â†’    ) {
101â†’        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with linting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
102â†’
103â†’        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
104â†’        val errors = mutableListOf<LintError>()
105â†’
106â†’        VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
107â†’            .visitor()
108â†’            .invoke { rule ->
109â†’                ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, false) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
110â†’                    val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
111â†’                    errors.add(LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected))
112â†’                }
113â†’            }
114â†’
115â†’        errors
116â†’            .sortedWith { l, r -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
117â†’            .forEach { e -> callback(e) }
118â†’
119â†’        LOGGER.debug { "Finished with linting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
120â†’    }
121â†’
122â†’    /**
123â†’     * Fix style violations in [code] for lint errors when possible. If [code] is passed as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on
124â†’     * the path are taken into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
125â†’     *
126â†’     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
127â†’     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
128â†’     */
129â†’    public fun format(
130â†’        code: Code,
131â†’        callback: (LintError, Boolean) -> Unit = { _, _ -> },
132â†’    ): String {
133â†’        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
134â†’
135â†’        val hasUTF8BOM = code.content.startsWith(UTF8_BOM)
136â†’        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
137â†’
138â†’        val visitorProvider = VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
139â†’        var formatRunCount = 0
140â†’        var mutated: Boolean
141â†’        val errors = mutableSetOf<Pair<LintError, Boolean>>()
142â†’        do {
143â†’            mutated = false
144â†’            formatRunCount++
145â†’            visitorProvider
146â†’                .visitor()
147â†’                .invoke { rule ->
148â†’                    ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, true) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
149â†’                        if (canBeAutoCorrected) {
150â†’                            mutated = true
151â†’                            /**
152â†’                             * Rebuild the suppression locator after each change in the AST as the offsets of the
153â†’                             * suppression hints might have changed.
154â†’                             */
155â†’                            ruleExecutionContext.rebuildSuppressionLocator()
156â†’                        }
157â†’                        val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
158â†’                        errors.add(
159â†’                            Pair(
160â†’                                LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
161â†’                                // It is assumed that a rule that emits that an error can be autocorrected, also
162â†’                                // does correct the error.
163â†’                                canBeAutoCorrected,
164â†’                            ),
165â†’                        )
166â†’                        // In trace mode report the violation immediately. The order in which violations are actually found might be different
167â†’                        // from the order in which they are reported. For debugging purposes it cn be helpful to know the exact order in which
168â†’                        // violations are being solved.
169â†’                        LOGGER.trace {
170â†’                            "Format violation: ${code.fileNameOrStdin()}:$line:$col: $errorMessage (${rule.ruleId})" +
171â†’                                canBeAutoCorrected.ifFalse { " [cannot be autocorrected]" }
172â†’                        }
173â†’                    }
174â†’                }
175â†’        } while (mutated && formatRunCount < MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE)
176â†’        if (formatRunCount == MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE && mutated) {
177â†’            LOGGER.warn {
178â†’                "Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file " +
179â†’                    "${code.filePathOrStdin()} in $MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE consecutive runs of format."

181â†’        }
182â†’
183â†’        errors
184â†’            .sortedWith { (l), (r) -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
185â†’            .forEach { (e, corrected) -> callback(e, corrected) }
186â†’
187â†’        if (!mutated && formatRunCount == 1) {

190â†’
191â†’        val formattedCode =
192â†’            ruleExecutionContext

202â†’        }
203â†’    }
